metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC verifying that all callers of `_assemble_context` (beyond `gate_ops.py`) have been updated to the public name, e.g. via a codebase grep/audit step or a test that the old private name no longer exists.
findings.finding: The 'sweep other callers of `_assemble_context`' described in the remediation's cascade note is uncovered work — no acceptance criterion verifies that all other call sites were updated.
findings.location: Remediation section — cascade_flag note; Acceptance Criteria (all three ACs)
findings.suggested_fix: Add: '- [ ] No remaining references to `operations._assemble_context` or `_assemble_context` exist outside of `operations.py` itself (verified by grep or equivalent).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that verifies no remaining cross-module references to `_assemble_context` exist (e.g., a grep/AST check or a test-suite import assertion).
findings.finding: The cascade_flag instructs a sweep of all callers of `_assemble_context`, but no Acceptance Criterion verifies that sweep is complete — an agent can satisfy all ACs while leaving other cross-module private references intact.
findings.location: Remediation section — cascade_flag note; Acceptance Criteria (AC list)
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: '- [ ] No `_assemble_context` cross-module import remains anywhere in the codebase (verified by `grep -r \'_assemble_context\' src/` returning only the definition site).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC #2 to cover ALL external callers: confirm `review_workflows.py:53` (and any future hits of `operations._assemble_context`) are updated to the public name alongside `gate_ops.py`.
findings.finding: `review_workflows.py:53` is a second production cross-module caller of `operations._assemble_context` (private name), but the plan's AC and remediation body mention only `gate_ops.py`. The cascade_flag hints at a sweep but leaves the scope ambiguous, meaning the rename could ship with one caller still using the private underscore name.
findings.location: Remediation / cascade_flag; AC line 2 (`gate_ops.py imports the public name`); `src/rebar/llm/review_workflows.py:53`
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC: '`review_workflows.py` and any other cross-module caller of `operations._assemble_context` also import the public name.' Update the cascade_flag to explicitly list `src/rebar/llm/review_workflows.py:53` as a required update site.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC line requiring `review_workflows.py` (and any other cross-module caller surfaced by a grep for `_assemble_context`) to be updated to the new public name, not just `gate_ops.py`.
findings.finding: The plan's cascade_flag notes 'sweep other callers of `_assemble_context`', but the AC only names `gate_ops.py` as the file to fix. A grep confirms a second cross-module private caller: `review_workflows.py:53` also calls `operations._assemble_context(...)` with the leading-underscore name. The AC as written would leave this caller broken or still importing the private name after the rename.
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: '- [ ] All cross-module callers of `_assemble_context` (currently `gate_ops.py` and `review_workflows.py`) import the promoted public name; no leading-underscore cross-module references remain (verified by grep).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/review_workflows.py` (line 53, `operations._assemble_context`) to the list of call sites that must be updated to the public name in the remediation and ACs.
findings.finding: `src/rebar/llm/review_workflows.py:53` is an unenumerated cross-module caller of `operations._assemble_context` that lives outside `workflow/` and is not mentioned in the plan's remediation or ACs; renaming `_assemble_context` without updating it will leave a broken private-name import.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC bullet 2 to: "`gate_ops.py` and `review_workflows.py` import the public name (no leading-underscore cross-module import)."

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC explicitly requiring that `review_workflows.py` (and any other file identified by the cascade sweep) also imports the public name — or, if the rename removes the private symbol, add a test confirming `operations._assemble_context` no longer exists.
findings.finding: The Acceptance Criteria validate only `gate_ops.py`'s import of the public name, but `review_workflows.py:53` contains an identical cross-module private import (`operations._assemble_context`) that the cascade_flag mentions in advisory prose. As written, the plan's ACs can be fully satisfied while this second caller retains the private import, leaving the encapsulation defect half-fixed.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (AC 2) vs. cascade_flag advisory prose
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: '- [ ] `review_workflows.py` and all other cross-module callers identified by the cascade sweep use the public name (verified by grepping for `_assemble_context` across the repo returning zero cross-module hits after the change).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate and update the three test sites that monkeypatch `operations._assemble_context` by its private string name (`test_completion_child_scope.py:70,92`, `test_completion_verifier.py:349,355`) in the plan's cascade sweep and add them to the ACs.
findings.finding: Three test files monkeypatch `_assemble_context` by its private string name (`monkeypatch.setattr(operations, '_assemble_context', ...)`); these are unenumerated consumers that will silently stop patching the real function after the rename, causing tests to pass against the un-mocked original or to raise `AttributeError`.
findings.location: Remediation / cascade_flag / AC: 'Existing completion-gate behavior unchanged (tests green)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC bullet: "Test files that `monkeypatch.setattr(operations, '_assemble_context', ...)` are updated to use the public name `assemble_context`."
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State a default implementation choice in the Remediation section (e.g., 'prefer direct rename to `assemble_context`; use a wrapper only if callers outside `gate_ops.py` depend on the private name and a rename is too broad').
findings.finding: The Remediation section presents two mutually exclusive implementation choices ('rename to `assemble_context` OR add a thin public `build_gate_context` wrapper') without stating a default or selection criterion, forcing the executor to make an unguided design decision.
findings.location: Remediation section: 'rename to `assemble_context` or add a thin public `build_gate_context` wrapper'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the OR clause with a stated default: 'Rename `_assemble_context` to `assemble_context` directly. If other callers of `_assemble_context` exist outside `gate_ops.py`, emit a thin public wrapper `assemble_context` that delegates to the retained private function and update only `gate_ops.py` to import the wrapper.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a requirement to update `operations.__all__` to include the promoted context-builder name as part of the public contract AC.
findings.finding: The plan's AC for promoting `_assemble_context` to a public function does not require adding the new public name to `operations.__all__`, leaving the module's public contract incomplete: star-import consumers and introspection tools will not see the promoted symbol.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC line 1: '…and the promoted name is added to `operations.__all__`'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly commit to either (a) an expand-contract sequence (add public name first, migrate callers, remove private) or (b) a single-step rename with an explicit justification that it is safe, and document a rollback path (e.g., revert commit or re-add the private alias temporarily).
findings.finding: The remediation offers two implementation options (rename to `assemble_context` OR add a thin `build_gate_context` wrapper) with different compat consequences, but neither commits to an expand-contract sequence nor provides a rollback path, despite the cascade note acknowledging multiple callers that will be affected.
findings.location: Remediation section — 'rename to `assemble_context` or add a thin public `build_gate_context` wrapper'
findings.suggested_fix: Prefer the expand-contract approach: (1) add `assemble_context` as a public alias alongside `_assemble_context`; (2) update all callers; (3) remove `_assemble_context`. Each step is independently revertable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the cascade sweep to include `operations._default_reviewer_id()` in `review_workflows.py:52` — either promote `_default_reviewer_id` to a public name or note it as out-of-scope with explicit justification.
findings.finding: `review_workflows.py` contains a second private cross-module call (`operations._default_reviewer_id()`) that is structurally identical to the gate_ops leak, but the plan's cascade sweep only names `_assemble_context` callers — leaving this sibling private-import antipattern unaddressed by the remediation.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `_default_reviewer_id` → `default_reviewer_id` to the promotion list in AC line 1, and add the corresponding import fix in `review_workflows.py` to the scope of the cascade sweep.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the plan whether `completion_precheck` should catch context-assembly errors and return a structured FAIL verdict, or let them propagate — and add a corresponding AC or note.
findings.finding: The plan specifies no fallback behavior for `assemble_context` (the promoted function) raising an exception inside `completion_precheck` — currently gate_ops has no try/except around the call, and the plan's AC only checks 'behavior unchanged (tests green)', not the error path.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for each AC: e.g., AC1: `grep -n 'def assemble_context' src/rebar/llm/operations.py` + `python -c 'from rebar.llm.operations import assemble_context; assert assemble_context.__doc__'`; AC2: `grep -rn '_assemble_context' src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_ops.py` (must return zero lines); AC3: `pytest src/tests/llm/test_gate_ops.py -v` (or the relevant test path).
findings.finding: No concrete proving command is provided for any of the three acceptance criteria — AC1 and AC2 rely on unstated grep/inspection steps, and AC3 names no test command or test file, leaving completeness unverifiable by a defined artifact.
findings.suggested_fix:
Append a 'Proving Commands' subsection:
```
AC1: python -c 'from rebar.llm.operations import assemble_context; assert assemble_context.__doc__, "missing docstring"'
AC2: ! grep -n '_assemble_context' src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_ops.py
AC3: pytest src/tests/llm/ -k gate -v
```
